riddle:
1. Seek the Wikipedia page whose web address ends with the three-word name given here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Stanley_Rogers; in its Early life and education paragraph he is described as the oldest of how many children — report that count as a number.

2. There exists a small numeric task next: take the number you found in clue 1 and add all of its digits together; what single number results from that digit-sum?

3. What elevation above sea level is recorded for the historic farmhouse whose page gives coordinates 39°33′34″N 81°34′13″W (i.e., 39.55944, -81.57028)? Use those coordinates to obtain the elevation in meters or feet and give it as a number.

4. If you follow the river-related link from that house’s article you will reach the little Ohio village that sits on the riverbank; consult that village’s entry and report the area figure shown in its summary infobox (as a numeric value).

5. The article whose opening mentions the river will lead you to the waterway itself; for that river, how many churches can you find located near its course? Perform a local places search and return the count as a number.

6. In the short local-history account you will encounter the old Mason family residence near the village — open its entry and write down the area value listed in its infobox (numeric).

7. The cartographer speaks plainly: compute (clue 2 + clue 3 + clue 5) % 10 and present the result; remember the final answer must be a single digit (0–9).

metadata.theme: A short local-history walk: starting from a Rogers namesake and tracing the small‑town fabric of Coal Run, Ohio — its historic houses, river corridor and connecting roadways.
